title: From 'goli maro' speeches to 'corona jihad': 7 hate speech cases Supreme Court dismissed
description: The Supreme Court on April 29 delivered a detailed judgment on a batch of long-pending petitions relating to hate speech through the media, election campaigns and social media narratives. [Ashwini Kumar Upadhyay v. Union of India & Ors]A Bench of Justices Vikram Nath and Sandeep Mehta clarified the scope of judicial powers in regulating hate speech, holding that existing criminal law provisions are sufficient to address such offences and that courts cannot create new offe
